J1.basis: the page content was 100% "Lumina" (AI meeting assistant) but `layout.tsx` metadata still said "InboxZap — Clean Your Inbox in Minutes." ... residual brand equity carries over
J1.rationale: Explicitly identifies the pivot (live=meeting assistant, GSC/repo=legacy InboxZap), states the implication for the low CTR, and frames the brand/FAQ/redirect fixes around it. Falls just short of anchor_10 only because it doesn't frame the growth strategy as fully as it frames the diagnosis.

J2.basis: 12 indexed blog URLs (`/blog/post-1`...`post-12`) now 404 per GSC — added 301s to `/blog` instead of leaving indexed URLs as soft-404s
J2.rationale: One blanket disposition (301 all twelve to the blog index) with only a generic 'still getting impressions per GSC' — no per-asset impressions/positions cited and no harvest/repurpose/keep/sunset differentiation, so it barely rises above the anchor_0 blanket instruction.

J3.basis: Send them to the live blog instead of soft-404ing indexed URLs ... added `alternateName: InboxZap` ... a bridge answer for "is this the same as InboxZap?"
J3.rationale: Avoids both failure modes — preserves legacy equity via 301s + brand bridge while building the meeting-assistant blog cluster as the growth engine — but never articulates the tension explicitly or sequences the transition, so it lands a defensible middle path without the deliberate reasoning of anchor_10.

judges.error: no JSON object in judge response

J1.basis: the page content was 100% "Lumina" (AI meeting assistant) but `layout.tsx` metadata still said "InboxZap"... added `alternateName: InboxZap`... a bridge answer for "is this the same as InboxZap?"
J1.rationale: Explicitly identifies the pivot (live = meeting assistant, GSC = legacy inbox demand, repo = partially stale metadata), states the click-loss implication, and frames every fix around the Lumina reality with a deliberate brand bridge. Falls just short of a perfect 10 only because the framing is embedded in fix-notes rather than stated as a standalone strategic premise.

J2.basis: added 301s to `/blog` instead of leaving indexed URLs as soft-404s... `source: '/blog/post-:id(\d+)', destination: '/blog'`
J2.rationale: One blanket 301 sends all 12 legacy posts to the generic blog index with no per-asset walk, no cited impressions/positions, and no harvest/repurpose/keep/sunset distinction — the GSC reference is a hand-wave ('still getting impressions per GSC'), not data-grounded disposition. Better than ignoring or deleting, but far from asset-by-asset triage.

J3.basis: so that residual brand equity carries over instead of confusing clickers... Send them to the live blog instead of soft-404ing indexed URLs... Replaced with 6 server-rendered posts on relevant keyword targets (meeting notes, action items...)
J3.rationale: Avoids both failure modes — doesn't chase inbox-cleanup content and doesn't torch equity (brand bridge + 301s + new meeting cluster as growth engine). But the tension is never articulated as a strategic choice, and redirecting inbox-era posts to a topically unrelated meeting blog is a weak equity bridge with no sequencing of the transition.
